title: Use curl to get repo file (fix #2714)
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind documentation

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
Replaces `wget` with `curl` in CentOS instructions for getting a more up-to-date buildah install. `curl` is included on CentOS minimal installations, `wget` is not.

This is a very small change, but may improve the install/upgrade experience for some people.

#### How to verify it

* Spin up a CentOS 7 minimal or CentOS 8 minimal VM or container
* Follow the steps for adding the appropriate Kubic project repo, under CentOS on `install.md`

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

<!--
Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Uncomment the following comment block and include the issue
number or None on one line.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`, or `None`.
-->

Fixes #2714 


#### Special notes for your reviewer:
I know this is a trivial change, but I wanted to make the process as error-free as possible.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: LGTM but a possible nit (maybe a nit with podman.io and not here): Just that podman.io/getting-started/installation.md mentions a lowercase `-o` while this has an `-O`. Maybe others can chime in on which one's better or if it makes any difference, but best to have consistency across the board.

The example on https://github.com/containers/podman.io/blob/master/getting-started/installation.md uses `-o` to specify the full path, the instructions here have the user `cd /etc/yum.repos.d/` first.

The example here could use `-o`, specify the full path, and drop the `cd /etc/yum.repos.d/` line. I have no strong opinion either way.

Your PR is a little messed up.

Could you squash your command and repush

git rebase -i origin
git push --force

> The example on https://github.com/containers/podman.io/blob/master/getting-started/installation.md uses `-o` to specify the full path, the instructions here have the user `cd /etc/yum.repos.d/` first.
> 
> The example here could use `-o`, specify the full path, and drop the `cd /etc/yum.repos.d/` line. I have no strong opinion either way.

Ah ack, would you mind -o and the full path and dropping the cd. One less step sounds nice to me :) 

Also, please squash your commits into a single commit as well.

Thanks a lot! :)
@cherdt also, please make sure your final squashed commit has a signoff, otherwise the gating CI job will fail.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cherdt*, *lsm5*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
